element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player starts with this; no learning curve
added as capsule pickup to expand firepower variety
mutually exclusive with laser; requires tactical choice
survive ship destruction; up to 2 in NES version, 3 in Famicom
culmination of stage patterns; power meter disappears before fight

typical_relief_triggers: reaching safe zone before boss arena
typical_tension_triggers: loss of power-ups on ship destruction
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: alternating horizontal-vertical stage structure creates rhythmic perspective breaks; boss encounters punctuate tension; power-up dependency drives moment-to-moment tension cycles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 introduces basic mechanics with manageable enemy density; horizontal scrolling establishes baseline control
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-4 escalate enemy patterns and introduce vertical scrolling perspective shifts; power-up combinations become critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 5-6 combine dense enemy waves with organic threat patterns (stomach muscle zone, brain attack); boss difficulty spikes significantly
notable_spikes: Stage 1 Golem boss marks first major skill check
Stage 5 wall collapse sequence escalates environmental hazard pressure
Final boss requires mastery of all power-up combinations and dodging patterns

notable_level_patterns.description: Six stages systematically alternate between horizontal and vertical scrolling perspectives, forcing continuous re-calibration of player spatial awareness and control muscle memory

NES Life Force version redesigns all backgrounds and enemies with organic/biological aesthetics (stomach muscle, brain regions); arcade Salamander uses mechanical/starfield. Creates thematic stage identity progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage progression from bio-entry to bio-mechanical brain

notable_level_patterns.description: Game progression depends on accumulating and preserving power-up capsules; loss of ship loses most upgrades but options survive, creating risk-reward tension on destruction events
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any stage; capsule pickups dictate survivability in next boss encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: Power meter visibility varies by stage before boss fights; disappears at different timing points (screen stop, boss appearance, wall collapse) creating disorientation during boss intros
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 1: meter hides when background disappears; Stage 5: hides when wall collapses
